commands.review_summary:
The PR adds cooperative yielding and abort checks while processing dense Ollama NDJSON streams, plus a regression test for timer progress during dense stream processing.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +41. Total +82 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the narrowed native-Ollama dense-stream path by source inspection: current main drains parsed NDJSON records without a cooperative macrotask yield, and the PR adds a focused timer-progress regression test. The full Windows local-daemon repro for the broader issue was not rerun on this branch.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR with no concrete repair finding; a human maintainer should decide whether to land it as a scoped mitigation while keeping the broader issue open.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- ports the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 onto a current maintainer branch, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yields and checks aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- adds a regression test that proves timers can run before dense stream processing completes

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate against the rebased maintainer branch, focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree, and earlier Ollama Cloud live smoke with `gemma3:4b`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts --reporter=dot
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --timing-json --idle-timeout 60m --ttl 120m --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: focused local wrapper passed `extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` with 11/11 tests after the latest rebase at `c0f574bf4f`; AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_5197dbab44d2`, run `run_9c7bfc7ea604`, exit 0. Earlier live proof on this PR also passed the Ollama Cloud fixture: AWS Crabbox `run_20baec24925c`, 2 passed / 2 skipped.

Observed result after fix: dense stream unit coverage confirms timer progress during parser drain, and changed extension type/lint/import-cycle guards passed remotely.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This is one mitigation in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, not the final issue closeout by itself.
target.author: vincentkoc
